title: Pluchea lanceolata
taxonomy: Pluchea lanceolata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the tribe Inuleae, which is a group of plants that are often shrub-like or herbaceous. Within the tribe Inuleae, Pluchea lanceolata is placed in the subtribe Plucheinae. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions, with a wide distribution across various parts of the world. Its classification is further supported by molecular studies, which have confirmed its placement within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Pluchea lanceolata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help in treating infections. In traditional medicine, Pluchea lanceolata is used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Pluchea lanceolata for medicinal purposes.
